review_text: Awesome park, great location and very friendly staff. They were even kind enough to store some of my gear while I was off hiking! Well worth the stay!
Had a one night say.  Cabin was clean and had everything we needed.|Park seemed full but was quiet.|Nothing to fault.

Great position.
Cabins are great.
Staff very friendly.
Would come back.

Beautiful location. The staff were very friendly.
After hours arrival process was super easy. The facilities were great, 2 bedroom cabins fit 6 (queen bed and 2 bunks). The cabin were super clean with good heating. The bathroom seemed to be recently renovated with a super large shower, although the shower head was quite small for the space so had to move around to get washed.
Short walk to the beach (2mins) and to the pennicott tour (15mins), ice creamery (20mins) which was great.
Iluka tavern, shop and Cafe at the beach front park of the park, access via a little path. Meals were pretty good!
The location is great, but the room has a constant sour, unpleasant smell. Even with the windows open all day, the odor doesn’t go away.
Close to everything.
Pretty standar

Kids Friendly
Amazing set up.
Heavenly View

We stayed one night here on our way north, sharing a cabin with friends. The cabins are well equipped, with a large living area. We are all from Newcastle and follow Newcastle Knights NRL team. Their match was on the Saturday afternoon and we were eager to watch it. Unable to get reception, we checked with the manager and he came straight away and replaced a cable. We would like to give him a big shout out, and our team won!

Great location & clean cabin. Only no WiFi - need to go main kitchen which is scary to walk at night ( no light provided along the paths in the park of Iluka). Good if planning to stay away from busy-ness and want a quiet me-time place.Surrounded by nice scenery . A great choice at Freycinet & near Coles Bay. Comes with kitchenette - can cook own food.
A very nice first time experience staying 3 days 2 night in a caravan park.. We stay in a two bedroom cabin. All basic facilities are available there. It is just a few minutes walk to the beach..

We had a lovely on night stay at this Big4! They were truly lifesavers going out of their way to find a last minute spot for us to stay and recharge for the night! There was a spacious camp kitchen and good WiFi for us to watch a movie at night. Great hot showers and a wonderful location to explore Freycinet National Park!
Beautiful location and lovely caravan park Highly recommend the boat trip to wineglass bay Awesome fishing on the jetty  Only negative terrible wifi

We booked a powered site, The facility is a bit dated but overall acceptable. The site itself is dirt and gravel. A very busy site.

The kitchen is reasonably well equipped with a kettle, toaster, microwave, tabletop oven, and cooktop. There are two BBQs available, though unfortunately they were quite dirty.
It seems some guests don’t clean up after themselves, which is disappointing, and there are no cloths provided to wipe down surfaces, making it harder to maintain cleanliness.

The bathroom needs to key to access which is good.  It was clean with powerful showers, although some of the taps could do with updating/fixing.
Tavern has tasty German style beers.. verandah over looks bay. Cottage accommodation is good. Bay glimpses though trees..from verandah that has clear roof.

The RV park is in a nice location and the facilities are generally clean, but a couple of things could really be improved.

The Wi-Fi is only available in the kitchen area, which isn’t very practical for guests staying in RV sites. It would be great if the park invested in a proper modem/router setup so Wi-Fi could reach the camping areas.

Also, access to the toilets and showers requires a key. This can be inconvenient, especially at night or if you forget to bring the key with you. A keypad or easier access system would make things much more comfortable for guests.

With a few upgrades like better Wi-Fi coverage and more convenient bathroom access, this park could be a much better experience for RV travelers.

Huge facility with cafe, tavern and IGA just a walk away.
One negative for anyone with mobility issues, there's a lot of uneven gravel roads to navigate around otherwise the serenity,ocean views, close proximity to a stunning beach and lots of shady coastal trees make the place definitely worth a visit to the Freycinet Peninsula.
Great spot, nice and quiet. Wonderful staff. Tavern is perfectly located next door.

We wanted to stay 4 nights but could only get in for 2 nights. From arriving and being greeted by the friendly receptionist who was lovely and welcoming. We had a great site 123, easy to get into plenty of room to reverse in. Wide and long enough to stay hooked up if your van isn't too big. We even had ocean glimpses.
The site was grass which we prefer, close to a small ablutions block 2 toilets and two showers, the main ablutions were further away with a laundry and plenty of clothes lines, which is a good thing as they don't allow personal clothes lines. The toilets were always clean and the park was well maintained.
I wouldn't have wanted to be by the bouncy pillow as the inflation mechanism was quite noisy and it was on all day. I only noticed it when doing my laundry.
The park is close to the esplanade with a lovely walk to Coles beach or further into the NP. There's a little grocery store, cafe and tavern easily accessed by a path from the park. I loved the park and could have stayed there longer.

Nice site with good sized spaces.
Great amenities.
Very close to the National Park and Muir's Beach for a swim afterwards.
Very well located park for all attractions.

環境不錯，公共設施還有一些給小孩的設施
尤其是跳跳床，小孩愛得很
房間內的設備也還算 OK
位置距離商店、餐廳、海灘都不遠，步行可抵達
整體給人感覺很不錯~
Saffire sits opposite us in the same bay. So you can't better this location. Very clean and walking distance to town
We loved our little cabin home. You can walk to pub, groceries and the beach easily.

The most uncomfortable lounge seats I'd ever encountered in our otherwise perfectly adequate cabin accommodation. If following Google Maps directions be aware that it directs you down The Esplanade. There is a tiny run up to the park reception to the left of the petrol station come general store. It isn't very well signposted and is easy to miss. DAMHIK.

Great location, lovely staff.
Toilet and shower block were outdated and not very clean.
Close to beach, shop, pub and cafes.
Overall a good place to stay.

We had stayed once before at BIG4 Iluka on Freycinet so knew what to expect.  Checkin was friendly and easy.   Our 2BR cabin was a good size and comfortably furnished.   The kitchen was well equipped for independent travellers.   The bed was comfortable.   The shower had plenty of hot water.   Aircon was good.  Wifi was poor but we had reasonable 5G.   Convenient to the beach, cafe and restaurant.

Powered site was located close to camp kitchen, the high kerb for reversing caravan was a nuisance but managed.  Staff in reception were exceptionally good, professional and friendly.  Amenities were very clean.  Camp kitchen was well set out.  Laundry had plenty of washing machines and dryers.  Definitely recommend others to stay here.
Campingplätze sind großzügig , i sehr gepflegt und die Sanitären Einrichtungen sehr sauber.

What you’d expect from a cabin in Big 4. One large space with lounge/bed/kitchen and state bathroom. Clean and tidy. Service was friendly and recommended walks to do prior to check in. The park was full when we arrived, so glad we booked a year in advance.

We stayed in a 6 bed cabin, it was clean and tidy but definitely is getting old. The lounge/dining area isn't very big and the seating wasn't very comfortable. Excellent location, close to Freycinet National Park, beautiful swimming beaches.
The lady that we talked to is a KAREN! So rude!NEVER AGAIN!

Being given a disabled access cabin at $200 a night that smelled like someone had died in it, made for an unpleasant 2 days stay. When reported to reception, there was no apology, no offer of another room and even after a suggestion of a cleaner to put some bleach down the drains, no offer of that either. Customer service lacking.

Perfect location, only a minutes walk to the beach, tavern, shop and Cafe. Beautiful long beach, never overcrowded even though campsite was busy. Even the campsite didn't feel suffocating. Lovely, friendly and welcoming staff all around. Clean and spacious cabins. Only thing was found the double bed and sofas a bit hard for our liking. Even with this we'd be back again. Kids playground was perfect for our toddler, he loved the blow up pillow/trampoline.
到達時已過了人員的辦公時間，幸好他們有WhatsApp 能聯繫到，人員也很熱情於電話了幫忙，非常感謝。房間也是很大很舒適的

This was an incredible spot! Showed up on the busiest week of the year with no reservations, but we were lucky enough to walk in and meet Rick, who found us a spot for 2 consecutive nights! Beautiful park, close to Freycinet Park, with a lovely cafe and restaurant about a 2 minute walk from our campsite! To top it all off, they even loaned us a hair dryer after ours caught fire!! Can’t say enough good things about the kind people in Tasmania; the people at this campground, however, are next level!

We stayed in a cabin here - it is just outside the national park in coles bay. It has its own general store, petrol and pub and is directly across from a beach and boat ramp and 10mins drive to wineglass bay walk. Very child and family friendly.
We called for a larger group booking and Cathy worked hard to make sure we all fit. Service, cabins and facilities were all great.
A wonderful park with plenty of cabins and camping spaces||Good location and very friendly and knowledgeable staff||Easy access and parking adjacent to our cabin

Well located near beach, complex has a shop, cafe ,pub and mixed camping options to suit most accommodation requirements. Having a basic cabin was comfortable for a short stay, adequately equiped and clean. Staff were friendly and helpful and amenties good.

Stayed 2 nights in a cabin and everything was A1
Nothing was a bother and everyone was very helpful 😊
I would recommend this to all my friends and family

Tasmanian first family tripped together before New Year 2026, stayed here for 3x nights and enjoyed fantastic places we went & explored. But turns sours at the end after checking out & have few more trips around the area.Then, head down to pick-up the other car we left at the holiday park (Big 4 Iluka). But when I went tto their toilet to make a quick pee near the reception. I was apprehended by this rude guy as in no manner at all & aggressively postering wants to harm me to the point.  That's why I asked him if you want you can the police . I explained i just  made I quick pee on the toilet  that all. Apprehended  just for that using the toilet ridiculous. I will never to come back to this place again Rude & no manner at all when talking to their post guests. Be aware some staffs have bad manners. The one running this place needs training their staffs.
Grumpy and unhelpful

The positives:
- Good location to the NP and food options (next door, with 2!). Didn't have options on which cottage we could get, so we ended up with a 6 bedder for 3 of us.
- The heating in the cottage was simple to use and works (since we had a cold rainy night).
- After hours check-in was simple and straightforward.
- The presence of WiFi in the camp cooking area helps, as do the kids playground!
Could be improved:
- Size of shower for 6 bedder should have been bigger, and there's barely any towel hanging space for 3 of us.
- Appreciated the heating in the main bed room, but the bunk beds should have that too!
Super gelegen! Freundliches und hilfsbereiten Personal! Gern wieder!

Great. Loved the place and the service.
We were on a caravan.
Manager is rude

We booked a campsite, but wanted to upgrade to the hostel, receptionists were super helpful. They also stored day packs for us when we went on an overnight hike. The hostel facilities were clean and had all the necessities. Camp kitchen was lovely.
Opposite beach, adjacent to pub & shop. Nice play area for the kids. Some grass & shade trees. Some sites have half decent views.
Average
receiption is very friendly and ready to provide answers to our queries . Happy to get a couple of good night stay at Freycinet
渡假屋有小型超市，鄰近也有餐廳。免費泊車，去酒杯灣及火焰灣也很方便。

Large Cabin.,Very comfortable and
Central to all attractions. Helpful friendly staff.. i recomend staying here.

We were only here for 12 hours having arrived late into the evening and leaving by 10am checkout. Booked it through Hotels.com. The communication with the property on the run up was very good. Reception closes at 6. We informed them of our late arrival and instructions were emailed to us how to get our keys.  This proved very smooth and faultless. The cabin we had was very good. Very clean and well equipped for basic self catering. Each cabin has space for one car.  There is no WiFi in the cabin but there is WiFi in a communal area close by.  Coles Bay has a couple of small supermarkets that meets needs.  You are also just a five minute drive from the entrance to the national park.  Our biggest regret is that we couldn’t stay longer in Coles Bay but if we did, there is no doubt that our stay at Big4 Iluka would have met our needs. Would definitely recommend it.

This is a great caravan park … it gets 100% for flat sites … plenty of room and there are good amenities on site if needed.|I would have one wish … if all single vehicles with sliding door access could be put together … let them enjoy the nonstop slide and slam of that said door amongst themselves .

Never stayed anywhere before where there is a threat on the wall that if you don’t wash your dishes and empty bin etc that you will be fined $50!! I thought I was on holiday and that was the cleaner’s job?? The handrails need attention , to avoid guests getting massive splinters. The staff were friendly and helpful. For what we paid , we were disappointed with the comfort, view (surrounded by vans and units) and cleaning expectations put onto guests.

We were here for 2 nights to explore Freycinet national park. The first day we did the infamous wineglass Bay hike. The second day we decided to be a bit more adventurous and did the mount amos hike. The Iluka accommodation is well located near these treks and it has a very nice cafe Granite Freycinet where we had almost all our meals there. The pizza evening on Thurs- Sunday is not to be missed.

Lovely location and close to the National Park + nearby walks. Online booking was straightforward and reasonably priced. After hours arrival instructions couldn't have been easier. The only minor mishap was that the site we had been advised was ours was already occupied however easily fixed by the manager and enjoyed a pleasant evening. Deducted 1 star for the unnecessary and annoying speed-humps😉

Recently, we stayed at the BIG4 Iluka on Freycinet Holiday Park.  Upon arrival, the staff were very welcoming and friendly.  They gave us maps and pamphlets of things to do around the area. Great location with the Beach, Shops, Café and a restaurant within walking distance. The cabins were clean, neat and tidy.  We stayed for two nights.  We had to shift out of our Cabin
(My Fault due to booking error) to another Cabin. The staff were very understanding and helpful.  I would recommend this park to anyone travelling around Tasmania.
Great cabins! Cathy was so helpful. Good facilities. Very close to pub, coffee shop & store

Stayed at a powered site for 3 days, absolutely lovely caravan park, spotlessly clean, great location, lovely staff, a little on the pricey side , but most things are there, we will Defiantly be back !
Accessible to Wineglass Bay Lookout, better than my expectation once entered the facility.

This is one of the most affordable stays in the Freycinet region, especially for family travelling with children like me. I booked a 2-bedroom cabin in Sep 2025 for one night stay to visit Freycinet national park. ||The cabin was clean and cozy. Sofa was comfortable. Heater and huge TV in the cabin. Complete kitchen utensils provided. A bottle of complimentary fresh milk provided in the refrigerator. Electric blanket provided in the master bedroom but not in the double decker bedroom. The hotel provides clear instruction on late check-in on email when the office is closed in the evening. This was really helpful for me. I was able to retrieve the key to my cabin based on the instruction in the email. ||A few minor letdowns but did not impact our stay. No dishwasher machine in the kitchen (hotel requires guest to clean up all used kitchen utensils and kitchenware before checkout).  No washing machine in the cabin. Coin washer and dryer are available in the shared facility building for campervan users. No Wi-Fi available in the cabin, but we can live with our mobile data.  ||When I returned the survey feedback form to the reception during checkout, the staff was a little unhappy with my suggestion in the form to provide free Wi-Fi in the cabin. She explained that the management tried ways to install wi-fi, but not successful due to many trees in the compound affecting the connectivity, no idea how true it is. It was merely a suggestion to improve customer experience.
Schön angelegter Campground kurz vor dem Nationalpark. Alle Anlagen sind sehr sauber und ordentlich!
Very busy campervan park.
Lovely, friendly owners and clean amenities.  Best hot showers ever!

Location! Location! This park has a fabulous position facing the bay and is within walking distance to the Géographe restaurant which serves excellent meals, including wood fired pizzas. We stayed on newly laid concrete slabs in two motorhomes. Unfortunately the nearby amenities block was closed, so we had to walk further up the park. Clean bathrooms with good hot showers. Nice selection of games for use free of charge. Highly recommend for anyone exploring the Freycinet National Park.

Lovely caravan park across from the beach with the tavern, general store and coffee shop in front of the park. The amenities are clean and great hot showers. Also has a good camp kitchen.  We had a great grass site with plenty of room. Very handy to the National Park.
I stayed for two nights in a motorhome. It had a bush flavour. There is nothing to complain about here. Everything I need was close.
מעולה
Loved our stay here. Friendly staff, great playground and camp kitchen. Beautiful area.

The location is great, right behind the tavern and general store and in close proximity to the walking tracks along the Bay. I personally found the staff to be a little cold and the room was basic with a stove we couldn't get to work for us. The showers were good and the Queen size bed comfortable. It appeared to us the cabins were a little overdue for refurbs.
Very good kitchen, but must got unlucky to get here when the hot water in the bathrooms wasn't working. Also, parking area on bare soil made quite a mass inside the motorhome.

Older style cabin, with all amenities & clean & tidy. Fully equipped with all you need. Tavern on site with delicious menu. Very small little town close by. Freycinet National Park approx 5kms away, with a choice of breathtaking hikes, suitable for every level.
Great caravan park right in the middle of Coles Bay clean facilities and friendly staff
Great little Tassie caravan park, great central setting in Coles Bay, good clean amenities and camp kitchen, beautiful spot even in Winter.

Expensive park at busy times of the year.  WiFi service is poor, but the park manager said it would be due to the trees.  Good shower pressure, but no dry area so your stuff will get wet.  Didn't check out the camp kitchen.

地理位置方便！自駕遊的，旁邊就是油站，亦有補給物資的超級市場，cafe和美味的餐廳，一日三餐都都沒有煩惱。入住後會給度假村的地圖，教你￼找到你的屋子，屋旁有自己的車位。房間內廚房設備齊全，廁所乾淨，有兩間房間，一間是雙人床有電暖氈，另一間是兩張雙層床可供四人睡。 唯一最大的缺點是屋子內￼沒有Wi-Fi，它的Wi-Fi只有在屋外面度假村的某位置才有，餐廳也有，這樣就不太方便，始終預訂房子資料原先說包含的。那就享受一下旁邊的風景吧，這間房子很舒適，建議冬天晚上入住的，如要外出走走，有需要就帶電筒吧。
Good location, near the beach and close to wine glass bay. The place got nice and warm, the bed was a little too soft for me and it could use an update but that’s what you get for a holiday in stay.

We stayed in a 2BR holiday unit that was spacious and looked clean, if a bit more rustic than expected from photos. Unfortunately there was an odor to the place, faintly smelling of urine. We were also surprised to discover all the cleaning rules that weren't disclosed prior to arrival. For example, you were expected to wash, dry, and put away all dishware; wipe down surfaces; and take out all garbage. If guests are expected to do chores as part of their stay, that should be made clear beforehand but, really, this shouldn't be an expectation at all given the fees being paid for the stay. ||Suffice to say that this place just wasn't for us so we checked out a day early, ate the non-refundable charges for the second night, and paid to stay elsewhere.

very kid friendly, receptionist was nice and kind. explains the facilities and the area informatively and is very welcoming. place was big was and has ur own parking up front. recommend friends and family to come here for some quality time together.
The cabins are fantastic! And nice and warm during the chilly nights!
We turn up very late on a very cold night and they had left the heater on. We walked into a wonderfully warm cabin on a cold winter night.  Above and beyond service.

Pre booked 4 night stay, powered site, 2 adults, May. Park is facing Coles Bay on the Freycinet Peninsula nestled into a hillside. Sites are mostly in tiered rows with many currently being refurbished to include concrete slabs, others are grass or dirt. Site 153 was easy access but not level and big enough for the van car and awning. Amenities have been modernised and are kept clean. Key lock for access. Showers have great pressure and plenty of hot water, the basin taps were my only annoyance with being push down , giving a couple of seconds of water, insufficient to wash your hands properly and unable to mix for warm plus hubby says impossible to have a shave with pure hot water.
Laundry has coin operated machines and dryers as well as hills hoists outside.
Camp kitchen was well placed with a view and fitted with all the basic necessities.
Park is a great base for the National Park and beaches.
Super nice place, very spacious and easy to find. Its location close to Freycinet national park and key attractions! Highly recommended!

We felt a bit like we were in trouble checking in late, at 5pm. But the accommodation was fine. We had one of the cabins and it was clean and comfortable. Decent kitchen to cook your own meals. It's only a short walk down to the beach to watch the sunset, and finish off with a beer at the pub which is on site. The cafe was great the next morning too. We only had coffee but the menu was very enticing. Great friendly service also. Great location for exploring the Wine Glass Bay Region
Stayed at the cabin during our trip to wine glass bay. A bit aged interior and facilities. Surrounded by caravans so lots of noise at night.
Fantastic location.

We stayed in a two bedroom villa for two nights, family of five. It was warm, cosy and beds were comfortable. It was a great base to explore wineglass bay and the national park. Lots of good eating options in driving distance.

Really disappointed. Paid $40 rather than staying in a free camp as we had been staying in free camps and hadn’t had a shower in 2 days. Showers were stone cold and painful to even have a birdbath in as it was so freezing.

When I went to hand the key in and tell staff that I was disappointed regarding the showers, they did not apologise and instead snapped back at me stating we should have called the after hours number - I hadn’t done this as I thought that would have been for emergencies. Waste of money and left upset after being spoken to so rudely.

Very central location to all the attractions.
With a nice sheltered beach. Good food options near by if you don’t want cook your own and very friendly staff.

Only downside is having to use a key to go to the public toilets at any time of the day not just at night. It becomes very impractical when you have a few kids of different genders travelling with you.
Great location for a Big 4. Watch the entry and exit when towing a big van. It's tight. Please, more level sites that are not mud, and on an incline.

Great location, right next to local restaurants and pubs, and just a short walk to the foreshore and Wineglass Bay cruise terminal. The amenities, including toilets and showers, were clean and well-maintained. The staff were friendly and welcoming, and all powered sites had easy access to electricity and fresh water. We stayed at a powered camper site. Highly recommend!
Coles bay is a very scenic area, the park is well situated
Its got all the basic facilities  level van sites

Great location,friendly staff, the camp kitchen could do with a renovation(old stoves, no possibility to close the kitchen causing possums to come in). One key to the amenities is not a great system, especially when you are not travelling alone, adding a thing to worry about, code system would be better.
Best  place to visit

Old lady refused to give information to tourist unless you get a deal with her; however you can get a good service from a young front desk girl
Not recommended to buy
I was told to leave as soon as I didn’t get what they offered

캠빙장에. 숙소를 별도로 잡았음
그냥 그럭저럭

Wir hatten ein grossräumiges Chalet mit zwei Schlafzimmern, ein Wohnzimmer mit zwei Sofas und einem Tisch mit vier Stühlen, eine grosse Küche mit allem notwendigen Geschirr und Küchenutensilien und einem Badezimmer mit Wärmelampe. Zudem hatten wir einen gedeckten Parkplatz neben dem Eingang und eine Terrasse mit Tisch und vier Stühlen. Dem entsprechenden war der Preis angemessen.

We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin and it was huge, everything we wanted in the kitchen and we mostly self catered. Loved everything about it. Walking distance to everywhere also. Absolutely would return here for another visit.

Excellent camping, just a few minutes from the national park. We booked two powered sites for campervans. The place has great amenities, clean and tidy. Moreover, the owner was really helpful and kind to us. Highly recommended.

We had a powered site for two days costing $190.  The site was tiny for what we had and enclosed so we didn't have an outlook and it was also on the dusty road which had vehicles going up and down all day.  The worst part was the sewage stench from the amenities building.  It had us gagging, just terrible.  If we had paid $20 a night we would have been okay but not $95, very overpriced.

*We couldn't move to another site as they were fully booked which we were told on arrival.

Cleanest and quietest hostel I've ever been to. The hostel is cleaned every day and it shows!

Staff
Cathy was very friendly and gave us a map of the area when we checked in. Also the cleaning lady (sorry don't know the name) was very friendly and even helped me look for my lost hair clip.

Rooms
Rooms are simple (it is a hostel), but clean and tidy. Towels are provided.

Kitchen
Well equipped kitchen with labels that clearly say where is what. Plenty of cooking equipment to use and decent space in the fridge. It did not feel crowded cooking where somebody else was there.

Bathroom
Very clean and spacious, which is not always the case in a hostel.

Location
The Holiday Park is close to a tavern, restaurant and general store. There is a beach nearby, great for swimming and enjoying the sunset. Very relaxing atmosphere.

Will definitely recommend!

Excellent staff able to handle computer pricing glitch for a powered site and honoured booking price. Thank you.
Also management allowed a second vehicle on site without notice. Again thank you. Great stay.

Advertised on website 55 a night for two people which is still enough for some electricity for a night. Get here and it's 75 for a patch of grass squished in between another caravan and behind cabins no space. Save your money, if you don't need power and go elsewhere free camping with water down next town over. 75 is way too steep because there's a cafe and what not that you still have to pay for...
It supposed to be camping and affordable.
Had a meal here

Mosquitoes were relentless and the campsite we were put in was just a patch of dirt stuffed in the corner on a very steep gradient couldn’t sleep without sliding down like a slippery dip. Staff seemed confused whenever we talked to them, amenities very well kept and easy to use and very convenient. Location was fantastic. We paid for two cars but were told off when the cars were both parked in our assigned bay??
Good holiday park, hot nonpaid showers, hot water in the kitchen for washing dishes a big plus.  They also have a store and pub on site.

Beautiful location next to the beach. Facilities are clean but very basic, one small stove in the kitchen, having one key to the amenities for the whole group is annoying. Hundreds of mosquitos in the summer. Nice coffee just outside the camp.

We didn't enjoy our stay here. The kitchen had only one stove shared among many guests, no materials to clean dishes, and no clear instructions for using the BBQ. While there’s a shop nearby for essentials, it’s very expensive. The beach offers a pleasant evening walk, but it’s not suitable for swimming. The town itself has little to offer, apart from its proximity to the Wineglass Bay hike entry. At $48, the price is far too high for what’s provided. Additionally, the staff couldn’t offer guidance on activities in nearby areas like Bicheno (30 minutes away), which is disappointing given that the majority of their guests are tourists.
One of our best stays in TAS so far. Location is excellent and a great starting point to explore Freycinet NP.
Great park , close to tavern and supermarket

Great camping in a fantastic place.
We enjoyed our stay a lot!
Fantastic showers and a lot of facilities.
Can be packed on the weekend but if you‘ve read the first sentence you know why 😁

Staying at big4 freycinet tonight.  The music from the hotel is blaring and there is very loud yelling and screaming.  This is the worst night we have had in Tasmania so far. We would never have stayed here if we knew it was going to be like this..certainly would not recommend and wouldn't stay again

Convenient location and close to the national park. A general store, cafe/pizza and tarven just outside. We had a powered campervan site. Site itself was good with power, fresh water and dump pipe for each site. Amenities block was old but useable. Great hot water. Kitchen area was small and limited to two electric hot plates.

Iluka caravan park it's self is rather nice. It has lovely hot showers and bathroom facilities that are kept nice and clean. There's plenty of room in the camp kitchens with a park and lots of room for kids to play or ride bikes. The grounds are big but to be completely honest the price is a bit exhubetant for a caravan park. I would say with all honesty that it's definitely the most expensive in Australia. We've traveled extensively all around the country a good number of times. For three of us for ten days was going to cost over $1100 which for a campground is beyond ridiculous, even if it is a powered site. Needless to say we didn't stay that long. It's cheaper to stay over on the national park side (if you can get a spot as usually you have to go into a  ballot to get a spot, we were just lucky) especially if you have a caravan, as they have a few powered sites.

For people camping in tents there are a number of bathroom facilities on that side but no hot water however they are decent and are cleaned every morning. There are also taps to access clean drinking water spotted along the roadside. The beach on that side is absolutely stunning and usually a lot calmer and more shallow making it ideal for families with young children. I would highly recommend buying a national parks pass and staying a couple of nights in the park. If you are in tents the sites are more private that at the caravan park. It can also get a little windy at times but it puts you right on the beach front with a private access to the beach at each campsite. The snorkeling is fantastic with lots of sea life and you can swim to most of the connecting bays.  I also have to say Tassie turns it on in the evenings when you will see some of the most beautiful sunsets in Australia from this location!! And in the morning when the weather is warm and water is still it's nice to enjoy a morning walk along the beach and up the bush track to the shops to grab a coffee from the friendly people at the cafe.
And you have to try the ice cream!!!!

In conclusion the Freycinet peninsula is definitely something you should NOT skip if you are in Tassie and have the time to visit. From the wildlife and sea life to the camping and walks not to mention the multitude of activities for you and your family to choose from and enjoy.
I guarantee you will not be disappointed with this stunningly beautiful part of the island!!
Not a bad place, camp kitchen ovens are old and parts missing they are not really up to scatch for a big4 park. People speeding in the park is annoying.

Great central spot. Clean, warm and convenient. Lovely couple running the park with heaps of tips for the area. Walking distance to beach, tavern and coffee shop. Cabin was a good size for just us, would be a little cozy for 6, but for just us we spread out. Heater in bathroom was great. Was a bit of a tight squeeze in the bathroom. Lovely deck area. And car spot was right next to front door
A nice place to stay with your family. It has all the facilities available and the beach is just a few minutes walk from this place. Loved this place.❤️

Location is right opposite the beach. There’s a nice tavern, also a cafe that does pizzas some evenings and a general store all next door, super convenient!
The bathrooms were clean and space was lovely. Not too far from the national park. Only downside was the last evening a coach full of teenagers were there on a school trip and completely took over the kitchen and all BBQs so if you wanted to cook you were out of luck for the night.
Friendly staff, great location for visiting the area. Clean well kept site and amenities.
Camper for a night. People very friendly and there is a great pub and beach only a 5 min walk away!
New cabins with modern appointments.  Very convenient to Freycinet national park.  Great sunset across the bay!
Clean and comfortable stay with a fully equipped kitchen! Love that the entire place (including the bathroom) has heating for the winter. It's very convenient and near the beach and restaurants.
Location is great, and the cabin is clean and comfortable. Wish we had more time to stay a little longer. Highly recommend.

Check in was easy and the staff was very friendly.  I have stayed in other Big4 camps before that were considerably nicer.  The electric bed heaters did not work and the unit smelled very strongly of cleaning product, so bad that we had to leave the windows open all night despite temperatures just above freezing.  There are few options near Freycinet national park, but in retrospect I wish that we had paid more to stay at the Lodge within the National Park.
히터소리가 너무 크다
Awesome park with great facilities, very well set out.

Stayed in Cabin #9 for 4 nights. Very comfortable with great beds, open plan with full cooking facilities, great showers, heating, balcony with outdoor setting, undercover carport all set amoung leafy gardens. Close to, or next door to some V good eateries,  supermarket, fuel plus stunning walks and views of beautiful Coles Bay. Great base to visit local wineries, Wineglass Bay, tour boats etc. A must place to stay in Tassie, and we will certainly return
Great park, nice tidy sites and some newer amenities. Good little camp kitchen. Well positioned park with a Cafe and pub out the front. Would stay again.

Adelaide Campers.
It rained for 2 weeks out of our 3 week trip to Tasmania, therefore decided to go to East Coast and stay here for a few nights and enjoy the  "sun"

I am someone who likes to feel clean and fresh. Yes!! Camping can be a struggle for me sometimes.

I've been to a few campsites, and they are not up to my standards, but that's ok.

I do like a clean, non a run-down bathroom & camp kitchen, which is hard to find.

This place was absolutely brilliant. It blew my expectations and was perfect for my "ocd."

The bathroom was clean and well looked after. The kitchen is clean and has a great set-up, and laundry clean, too.

We had a great time here and had a hard time leaving Coles Bay and Tasmania.

Here, we had a spot with the view of the beach. It was peaceful and had a good vibe. We were able to go for nice walks, eat at the ice creamery (get the Lavender flavour ) walk to the beach, watch the sunset, head to the cafe and the pub for drinks and dinner.

This is absolutely a beauty of a spot.. It's a must stay. Hopefully, I'll return one day.

Thanks
Had a wonderful stay in the hostel. Staff were amazing, very grateful for a hot shower and a warm bed!
The bathrooms are okay. The showers don't seem to get too warm.

Hooray! We’ve hit the jackpot with this one. Terrific location, convenient to everything, great facilities, cheapest rates we’ve had so far, don’t pay for a shower AND they’re untimed, but best of all is the attitude of the managers. At the end of a long summer season in Tassie, most caravan park managers/owners we’ve struck are at the end of their tether. Not these guys! It’s like they’ve stepped fresh off the boat. Friendly, helpful and bouncing with enthusiasm, we couldn’t have asked for a warmer welcome. 👍
Great location, very good caravan park and very good value for money.
Great campground. Clean facilities, close to the pub (great meals). Book early around school and public holidays.
We received great service from Ian when booking and checking in. The sites vary quite a bit so you need to know the size of tent or camper.  Very good facilities and showers were first class.
Sehr guter Platz. Sehr freundliches Personal. Toiletten und Duschen ok.
Staying in a tent,  sites are not overly large but are ok.

Absolutely wonderful!
We had an extremely relaxing holiday based here  in a campervan - Awesome location, very clean amenities and friendly staff.
Everything you need is in walking distance, or  a short drive to the wonderful tracks of  Wineglass Bay and Hazards walks.
Highly recommend Iluka on Freycient.

Place is good for a short stay but the park is small layout quite wierd and if you were a camper youd be at the back of the park. Not a bad thing as its all Vans here. Staying in a cabin. Nice enough. Good value for the price so no complaints from me.
Great quite clean park.

The powered sites on level two are so small that you have nowhere to park your car. This is an oldish caravan park which is extremely tight to manoeuvre. Great setting though in a fantastic part of Tassie. On a positive note staff were friendly and helpful amenities were clean
Busy park. Clean amenities.

Stayed 3 nights here and had a wonderful time! The park managers, Emma and Ian, were lovely and couldn’t do enough for you. Ian helped me reverse into my site. Location is great for hiking, so close to Freycinet National Park. Also, the beach, cafe (with great coffee), pub and service station right next door.

Beautiful location overlooking the bay (which can be busy) & next door to a pub/bistro (often with live music).  Very friendly managers.  We had a pleasant, grassed powered site.  Amenities are plentiful & clean.
A wonderful part of Tasmania - the accommodation was lovely and staff were great- 100% recommend 😊👌

We stayed 3 nights in a recently renovated studio cabin which surpassed our expectations. Comfy bed, good size kitchen and sitting areas. The managers were friendly and helpful and there was a shop, cafe and tavern next to the site which catered for all our needs. Plus the beach was across the road and the bus stopped right outside. Internet was slow but we managed!

Staff did everything to accommodate getting our camper onto a cracking powered site without any booking. Even provided an extension power cord at no charge. Facilities clean. PS beautiful location. Will be back again.
For $85 per night you think they could afford to have shower curtains in the park amenities

The location is absolutely magical! Close to beautiful beaches Coles bay, Sleepy beach, Honeymoon beach and then of course Wineglass beach. Comfortable, clean but a bit dated cabins. Friendly and helpfull staff. Bar and eatery within walking distance a few 100 meters. Lovely little coffee shop / eatery for early morning coffee and breakfast.

We stayed in a unpowered campsite, kitchen is full equipped with fridge, microwave, toaster and small oven with stove. Toilets and showers (hot🤩) are clean and spacious
The lady at the reception was very lovely and kind by let us in at the last minute 🙌⛺️🚐
Definitely a place to come back

Location is truly magic in the campground which sits directly on Coles Beach which has a bakery, a good grocery store, a bistro, a bar, and a bottle shop.  Very good swimming beach. And of course the proximity to all the national park sites & hikes. Place is magic! Oh and great sunsets over the water too.
Stayed in a cabin - number 157. Amazing. Very modern and comfortable. Nice quite location. Super comfy bed. Nice deck to relax on, and everything is 5 mins walk away. Highly recommend.

Simply the best. We had a great time here and had a hard time leaving Coles Bay. Facilities here are excellent. Laundry was excellent given we had been off grid a fair bit as were the showers. Site we had was awesome, beach views from the top and then everything else is at your doorstep. Walks, runs, bike rides, fishing, exploring, food and coffee. This was a memorable stop to end 2023. Can’t wait to get back here. Staff were friendly and there is a good atmosphere to the park.
Loved it , its a great park everything close by
Spent a week here in December 2023 everything was fantastic perfect location to have adventures. Park managers Ian and Emma could not have done a better job!!!

5 fantastic stars.
Management and office staff were very helpful and welcoming from the moment we arrived until when we checked out.
We stayed in a family cabin, and it was great. Plenty of room for all to sleep with a double bed, which was a generous sized double, and two sets of bunk beds. We stayed over Christmas, and on our way there I remembered I hadn't packed the baking pan and salad bowls, but that was okay, as the kitchen had all basics that we needed to get through Christmas day cooking.
The cabin was fantastically clean when we arrived and huge bonus points as there were plenty of pillows. I use two, and it's frustrating when hotels only have one per person.
Laundry had plenty of washing machines and dryers for use, and we never had to wait to use one.
The cabin was located just near the childrens park and it was great being able to sit on the balcony and relax while still being able to watch the kids.
The holiday park is very conveniently located just behind the pub, general store/fuel station and a Bakery as well as a five minute walk from the beach.
Going back to the fact we were there over Christmas. 8:30 Christmas morning, Santa came around all the cabins/camp sites and handed out lollies. I thought that was a lovely thing to do as management/staff took time out of their day to bring a bit more Christmas cheer to the kids. Also, a shout out to the fire service for also visiting with Santa in the fire truck and handing out lollies. Our kids had a ball with Santa visiting twice.
Overall, we're very happy with BIG4 Holiday Park and Coles Bay in general, and we'll be visiting again.
Good clean amenities.

Excellent location across from a beautiful beach and with a small shop, pub and bakery a couple of minutes walk away. Slightly longer walk - 15 mins along the beautiful coast - is a great mini town with incredible food options
We usually avoid Big4 CPs but this park was a really nice place to stay without all the whistles and bells usually associated with Big4 parks and it was well priced. The staff were great also.
Wifi connection was unstable and the only downside.

Located close to everything you need. The 3bedroom cabin we rented was ideal for 3 fishermen but would also suit 2 families with up to 4 kids. Big lounge well set up kitchen and the cabins are well spaced so noise isn’t a problem. Friendly welcome and reasonable prices.

Fantastic cabin and great facilities. The cabin suprisingly had a large tv and a deck with. tables and chairs. Being a caravan park we were also able to use the lau dry facilities.

Big thanks to the staff who gave me a call just after we left to let me know that I'd left a few things behind in the room and that I could grab them from the office!
Cute little 2 bedroom cabin. Fully equipped kitchen. Friendly receptionist . Would recommend this place.
We had a powered RV site. Great facilities, comfortable, safe. The staff was very helpful. Great, hot shower included in price. Clean bathrooms close to camp.
Schöner sauberer campground  tolle Lage,

We stayed for only one night at an unpowered site but we will definetly come back! Very friendly and informative welcome from the receptionist and hearty farewell by the owner.
The park has a restaurant and bakery very conveniently closeby and as mentioned the showers are great!
No time limit, straight forward temperature adjustment and good pressure. Really appreciated luxury during a longer camping tour!
Nice holiday park. Clean amenities. Large sites. Very friendly and helpful staff.
Awesome ! Clean amenities, fantastic staff and greet location!

Perfect location for all things great to Freycinet National Park. We were greeted by the most loveliest lady upon check in. She provided us with all information we needed to know and much more around local knowledge spots etc. This all really helped us develop our plan of attack and further enjoy our experience in our short time here. Would definitely stay here again 🙌
You can't beat it, well located, many options for accommodation,  and situated next to tavern, general store, bakery.
Beautiful spot, great beach in front. Tracks into Coles bay.
We stayed here for 1 night in our camper van and wished we'd booked longer. Beautiful site with a bar, restaurant and cafe all 2mins walk away. Bathrooms were clean 👌

This was a great caravan park . We stayed here for four nights in a family cabin. We were happy with the area, space inside cabin. Very clean and staff were very friendly. |The only issue was the terribly uncomfortable couches and the Wi-Fi was non existent.
Friendly staff and tidy rooms with nice beds.

Beds were comfy, we stayed in a cabin with two people.

Clean water, shower was warm.

Service was friendly and chatty with lots of info for walks in the national park and places to eat (what was open and not etc)
Good location and facilities, friendly staff.
Great location, next door to hotel, coffee shop and convenience store for all tourists to the area. Amenities were very clean and tidy.

깨끗하고 편리한 캠핑장이었습니다.
캠핑장 바로 아래 마트와 주유소 바가 있어서 좋았습니다. 해변도 바로 있어서 해지는 바다를 감상 할 수 있습니다.

Quality accommodation with super friendly staff. Close to the beach and amenities.

A perfect location for a trip.on the edge of Freycinet.

It’s a great spot. Lovely lodge/cabins with ample space for kids to play. There’s a petrol pump and grocery store right next to it. A couple of eateries and a lovely beach across the road. Loved the stay and the rooms thoroughly. Also well located.
Comfy beds, nice cabin with everything you need
Thanks again for looking after everybody you could during the bushfire emergency. You guys were just so helpful and kind. We will never forget this. Thanks and see you again soon.
Great location, with a pub and general store just outside. Decent bathroom, nice beds, friendly workers and great apliances like a stove and microwave in cabins. Would reccomend.
Couldn’t be more perfectly situated for access to the incredible Freycinet National Park. Great views over the water and all the facilities you need. Thanks guys!

Amazing place! I’m not a huge fan of holiday parks but this one is super pretty. The owners were extremely friendly. We stayed in a campervan and the sites are very big and well leveled (which is super important when you sleep in it!). The view from the kitchen space is amazing, would definitely recommend this place
Not pet friendly !
Lovely private cabin! Decke out with a nice big TV and electric blanket:) clean and tidy with everything we needed

Family of 4 stayed for 2 nights in 2-bedroom cabin. Cabin was large with a full modern kitchen. Beds were very comfortable. Would definitely stay again. Laundry was pretty pricy @ $5ea for the washing machine & dryer.
We have a wonderful stay at this campground ⛺️in June 2023. Equip with full kitchen facilities & spacious rooms. Come with strong wifi too in the room

Our 6 bed cabin at Illuka was okay, the cabin is tired and worn. There was mould in the bathroom, the curtains had so many holes in them and the heater didn’t heat up the space. We expected a little better for the average cost for this cabin.
